stem: A pebble is thrown from a bridge. The equation h = -4.9t2 + 11t + 25 represents this situation, where h is the height of the pebble above the ground, in meters, t seconds after it is thrown. According to the equation, what is the height, in meters, from which the pebble was thrown?
rationale: The height from which the pebble was thrown is the value of h when t = 0. Substituting t = 0 into h = -4.9t2 + 11t + 25 gives h = 0 + 0 + 25 = 25. Therefore the pebble was thrown from a height of 25 meters.
